description: Authoritative PASS/FAIL criteria for Compre Barato Alagoas visual + input QA. Pair with e2e/qa_matrix.json. Open before matrix_critique.md / video_critique.md. Adapted from vinys-toolbelt autonomous-dev-cycle (2026-07-17).

pre_prod_pass_requires: Every expected matrix cell has a CRITIQUE line
Every e2e recording has a VIDEO line
No open BAD where severity is blocker (or user accepted residual BADs in writing with IDs listed)
Artifact labels match actual GameState on the image/frame
Phase A handhelds used Android emulator + adb screenrecord + adb shell input (not Chrome emulate alone)
A4b video review checked input_simulation_scenarios expected_video_outcome for paths run this session
Normal SIM-* scenarios covered per modality used; open edge SIM-* BADs accepted or fixed
Every expected matrix PNG has this-run *.review.json (opened:true)
Playing reviews walked fairness_usability criteria (F-*) for handheld cells + F-NO-DESKTOP-REGRESS on desktop playing cells

critique_format.matrix_line: CRITIQUE {format_id}_{shot_suffix}: GOOD: <evidence> | BAD: none | BAD: {criterion_id}: <what fails> [; {criterion_id}: ...]
critique_format.video_line: VIDEO {format_id}_{keyboard|mouse|touch}: GOOD: <evidence> | BAD: none | BAD: {criterion_id}: <what fails>
rules: GOOD and BAD both required on every line
BAD: none only after opening the artifact(s) and checking every applicable criterion in this file
When failing, cite criterion id(s) from this file (e.g. V-PLAY-NESTED-BORDER)
Listing a directory or trusting a prior agent PASS is not review
Filename alone is not proof of screen state — verify GameState from pixels/text

severity.blocker: Must be fixed or user-accepted in writing before push (Phase B) or claim ship
severity.major: Ship blocker unless user accepts residual in writing with criterion IDs
severity.minor: Should fix; may ship only if user accepts residual in writing

anti_patterns: Marking BAD: none because a previous critique said so without re-opening THIS run's artifacts
Accepting *_04_playing.png that shows Menu / ModeSelect / GameOver
Ignoring Phase C open_bads / events=0 because Phase A critiques were green
Treating Chrome page.emulate handhelds as ship-valid Phase A
Reviewing only boot/menu and skipping playing + landscape
Calling double blue frames or OOB stars 'intentional' without code proof + product sign-off
Treating suite CAPTURE_OK / exit 0 / N/N as A7 or as permission to write BAD: none
Status line '15/15 PASS open_bads none' without opened CRITIQUE/VIDEO evidence for this run
Calling A5 VERIFY (files present) a visual review
Marking VIDEO BAD: none without checking motion/dash feedback across ≥20s play
Skipping edge simulations (dash spam, multi-touch, max stick deflect) entirely
Assuming keyboard works because mouse path CAPTURE_OK
Claiming A4b/A6 done without writing per-file *.review.json sidecars
Batch-writing all_good sidecars without opening each artifact
Rollup BAD: none while sidecar verdict is issues (or missing sidecar)
Fixing handheld fairness by changing desktop world speeds, PLAYER_RADIUS, or Desktop1080 view_height
Calling handheld play fair without checking F-* (play area, entity CSS size, cross-time, stick size) on this-run playing shots
Skipping phone_rodin_chrome / short-height landscape when judging resize fairness
Marking portrait menu/mode BAD: none while blue panel laterals are cut by the canvas edge (V-PANEL-IN-CANVAS)
Marking playing BAD: none while 'Dash X.Xs' / status text sits on the stick or DASH button (V-PLAY-HUD-CLEAR / V-OVERLAP)
Skipping mode_select V-PANEL-IN-CANVAS because menu was already reviewed

form_factor.description: Logical form factors for CSS viewports (not physical panel pixels).

classes.PhonePortrait: short side typically ≤430 CSS px, portrait
classes.PhoneLandscape: short side typically ≤440 CSS px, landscape
classes.Desktop4k: QHD/4K — comfortable, not sparse huge chrome

rules: Handheld: targets ≥48 CSS px; gaps ≥12 CSS px

global.pass: No clipped primary text (prices, store names, CTAs)
Type readable on phone landscape and laptop_hd
Critical UI not under notch/home indicator

results.pass: Honest empty state when no stores

share.pass: Share CTA reachable when in scope

checks.pass: Stills that fail V-* still fail video

artifact_criteria.description: Presence and honesty of QA artifacts.

checks.pass: Every cell CRITIQUE; every recording VIDEO

fail_if_any: Rubber-stamp (GOOD while known_fail_examples still on this-run artifacts)

agent_instructions: A7 must fail if any required sidecar is missing or opened is not true.
After opening each PNG or video, write sibling *.review.json (all good OR list what is wrong). Review incomplete without it.
Compare video frames to expected_video_outcome; CAPTURE_OK step counts do not prove motion/dash feedback.
Simulate or confirm e2e covers both normal use and edge cases — not only happy-path boot→play.
When reviewing VIDEO: walk input_simulation_scenarios (normal + edge) for that modality; cite SIM-* ids on BAD lines.
On playing cells: also walk review_checklist_fairness (F-*) — desktop vs handheld lists differ; cite F-* on BAD lines.
Never fix handheld fairness by changing desktop speeds/radii/view_height; prefer chrome insets, view_height_for(class), or mobile-only layout.
In status files, label capture vs review explicitly (CAPTURE_OK vs A4b/A6 reviewed).
CAPTURE_OK (scripts) ≠ REVIEW. Never mark BAD: none solely because capture printed PASS/CAPTURE_OK.
Before any A4b/A6 critique line: open scripts/qa_success_criteria.json and scripts/qa_matrix.json.
Open each artifact with the image tool (or sample video stills). Directory listing is not review.
For each screen, walk review_checklist_by_screen and mark FAIL with criterion id if any check fails.
If unsure whether a shape is intentional VFX, FAIL major and ask — do not invent BAD: none.
When this file and a skill text disagree on PASS/FAIL, this file wins for criteria; skills win for process/order.
Update this file when new regression classes are found (add criterion id + known_fail_examples) — do not only mention them in chat.

vocabulary.description: Two different meanings of PASS. Never conflate them.

CAPTURE_OK.means: Automation step succeeded: artifact written, state reached, steps counted, HTTP 200, matrix cell non-empty.
CAPTURE_OK.does_not_mean: Looks good, criteria passed, ship ready, A7 PASS.

REVIEW_PASS.means: Agent opened this-run artifacts, walked criteria, and recorded GOOD/BAD with evidence.
producers: human/agent image-tool review writing matrix_critique.md / video_critique.md

status_language: Say 'capture complete (CAPTURE_OK)' for runners — never 'A4 PASS' alone.
Say 'A4b/A6 reviewed; BAD: none' only after critique lines with opened-image evidence.
A7 is REVIEW only; CAPTURE_OK is a prerequisite, not the gate.

per_file_review_json.means: Agent analyzed one media file and wrote *.review.json with all_good or issues
per_file_review_json.does_not_mean: Capture succeeded or rollup line exists without sidecar

per_artifact_review.description: Review of a media file is complete only after the AI agent opens it and writes a sidecar *.review.json. Rollup CRITIQUE/VIDEO lines alone are insufficient.
per_artifact_review.why_json: Machine-checkable at A7; stable schema; easy to count missing sidecars.

schema.artifact: string path relative to repo root
schema.run_start_unix: number from status/run_start_unix for this pass
schema.opened: must be true — agent used image tool or sampled video/stills
schema.summary: all good OR short description of what is wrong
schema.issues: [] if all_good; else [{id, detail}] with criterion or SIM-* id
schema.sim_scenarios_checked: optional array of SIM-* ids for videos

completeness: File F is reviewed only if F.review.json exists with opened:true and valid verdict
A7 requires sidecars for every expected matrix cell and every required recording this run
verdict issues must not pair with rollup BAD: none for the same artifact/unit
Stale sidecars from older run_start_unix do not count

input_simulation_scenarios.description: Simulate real user input (normal play + edge cases). Capture VIDEO of each path. A4b review must compare frames/timeline to expected_video_outcome. CAPTURE_OK (script steps) is not enough — video must match expectations. Runners: e2e_inputs (kb/mouse), e2e_emulator_matrix / e2e_phone (touch).
how_to_review_video: Open recording or extracted stills across the timeline (not only first/last frame).
For each scenario id that the path claims to exercise, verify expected_video_outcome.
If outcome fails, VIDEO line BAD must cite scenario id and/or criterion id (SIM-*).
Matrix PNGs alone cannot pass SIM-* criteria — need continuous video/stills.

modalities.keyboard: Desktop/laptop primary: WASD/arrows move, Space dash, Enter/Esc menus
modalities.mouse: Desktop: left-drag point-to-move, right-click dash, clicks for menus
modalities.touch: Handheld: virtual stick + DASH button; taps for menus; two-finger back where defined

scenarios.simulate: User dismisses boot CTA once (Enter/Space or click/tap on CTA).
expected_video_outcome: Boot overlay disappears within ~1s of input
Main menu (RUSTY DASHER title panel) appears — not stuck on blank/WASM spinner
No double-advance past menu into mode select on a single dismiss

scenarios.simulate: User confirms main menu (Enter/Space or tap/click panel center).
expected_video_outcome: Transitions to SELECT MODE
All four modes visible or navigable
No panic/black frame

scenarios.simulate: User taps swap-controls band (bottom strip) then confirms into play; verify stick/DASH sides swapped vs default.
expected_video_outcome: Swap band reacts (or copy updates Stick LEFT/RIGHT)
In Playing, stick and DASH appear on swapped sides
Both still outside playfield

scenarios.simulate: User cycles all 4 modes and all 4 difficulties, then START.
expected_video_outcome: Selection highlight/cursor moves through Classic, Zen, Survival, Timed
Difficulty changes Easy→Insane (or compact chevrons)
START enters Playing with matching mode/difficulty on HUD

scenarios.simulate: Hold WASD/arrows ≥20s with direction changes; Space dash ≥2 times.
expected_video_outcome: Player (cyan disc) translates in pressed directions — not frozen
Dash produces visible burst/trail or cooldown text change (Dash READY → timer → READY)
No stick/DASH on-screen chrome
Score/hearts/HUD update or at least remain live (not frozen boot frame)

scenarios.simulate: Left-drag point-to-move across field ≥20s; right-click dash ≥2 times.
expected_video_outcome: Player moves toward cursor / drag direction over many frames
Right-click dash visible (trail/cooldown)
No virtual stick chrome
Continuous play not a still image

scenarios.simulate: Drag virtual stick in ≥3 directions over ≥20s; tap DASH ≥2 times.
expected_video_outcome: Stick knob offsets from base while dragging; returns toward center on release
Player moves matching stick direction
DASH press scales/highlights button; dash effect or cooldown visible
Stick + DASH stay outside blue playfield (Fortnite-style ring stick OK)
Portrait: controls in bottom margin; landscape: stick L grip, DASH R grip (or swapped)

scenarios.simulate: After Game Over, confirm again (Enter/Space/tap).
expected_video_outcome: GAME OVER / NEW HIGH SCORE panel visible first
Again restarts Playing or returns to a valid post-GO flow without blank/panic

scenarios.simulate: Back/Esc or two-finger / left-edge back to menu when reachable.
expected_video_outcome: Leaves current screen to Menu or ModeSelect as designed
No stuck GO overlay

scenarios.simulate: Double-dismiss boot (two quick Enter/clicks/taps).
expected_video_outcome: Ends on Menu or ModeSelect — not Playing skipped accidentally without mode UI
No crash; canvas still receiving input

scenarios.simulate: Tap/click far from CTA (corner) then on CTA.
expected_video_outcome: Miss does not freeze game
CTA hit eventually dismisses boot

scenarios.simulate: Micro-nudge stick inside deadzone (~<12% radius) then full deflection.
expected_video_outcome: Near-center nudge does not cause large player teleport
Full deflection moves player clearly
Knob does not leave outer stick ring

scenarios.simulate: Drag stick far past max radius (outside base).
expected_video_outcome: Knob clamps to ring edge (no knob flying across screen)
Player still moves in that direction at full strength
Release returns knob toward center

scenarios.simulate: First touch on stick-side deck/grip away from exact center (fat-finger grab zone).
expected_video_outcome: Stick engages (knob follows) without requiring pixel-perfect center hit
Does not accidentally trigger DASH

scenarios.simulate: Spam dash (Space / right-click / DASH) ≥5 times in 2s including during cooldown.
expected_video_outcome: No crash/panic
Cooldownhes only when allowed; cooldown text or button feedback appears
Player does not rubber-band out of bounds

scenarios.simulate: Multi-touch: hold stick deflection + tap DASH (two fingers).
expected_video_outcome: Both controls respond: movement continues while dash fires
No control swap/glitch; stick knob stays on stick side
Player dashes roughly along move facing

scenarios.simulate: Third finger tap on field / HUD while stick owned.
expected_video_outcome: Stick ownership not stolen into random teleport
No unintended menu exit mid-play

scenarios.simulate: Drag cursor to window edge / outside canvas then back.
expected_video_outcome: No panic
Player motion remains sane when cursor returns
No stuck dash or frozen input

scenarios.simulate: Hold Left+Right or Up+Down simultaneously, then release one.
expected_video_outcome: No crash
Motion cancels or prefers one axis consistently; release resumes correct direction

scenarios.simulate: Press Esc during play (if supported) or after short play.
expected_video_outcome: Either returns to menu cleanly or is ignored without freeze
Not a half-drawn hybrid HUD/menu

scenarios.simulate: Double-activate START.
expected_video_outcome: Single clean enter into Playing
Not two overlapping runs or missing chrome

scenarios.simulate: qa_matrix force game-over path (suite): play until forced GO.
expected_video_outcome: Real GAME OVER or NEW HIGH SCORE panel (not menu, not mid-play freeze)
Stats readable; form-factor hints correct
No clipped title

scenarios.simulate: Compare portrait vs landscape recordings for same phone class.
expected_video_outcome: Portrait: stick+DASH bottom margin (Game Boy)
Landscape: stick left + DASH right grips (PSP) unless swapped
Never stacked mid-field (rodin regression)

scenarios.simulate: Tap near midpoint between stick and DASH.
expected_video_outcome: Does not fire both or neither chaotically every frame
Hit targets remain ≥48 CSS px; gap ≥12 CSS px (geometry)
Video shows intentional single control when aiming at one

scenarios.simulate: After boot dismiss, press movement keys without extra click.
expected_video_outcome: Keys work in menu/play (canvas focused) — not dead keyboard until click
Player moves once in Playing without requiring a prior canvas click

scenarios.simulate: Continuous play ≥20s (suite default play_ms) with periodic move+dash.
expected_video_outcome: Recording length supports ≥20s play segment (or stills span that duration)
No freeze-frame after 2s of motion
No progressive HUD corruption or control disappearance

fairness_usability.description: Play-feel fairness and control usability across formats after resize. Desktop is the reference baseline; handheld must stay fair and usable without making desktop worse. Apply on playing matrix PNGs, mid-play e2e stills, and ≥20s video segments. Cite F-* ids on BAD lines.
fairness_usability.principle: Desktop play-feel (field size, entity readability, cross-field time, hazard density, no touch chrome) is the baseline. Handheld after chrome insets + view_height_for must remain fair and usable; do not regress desktop world constants or Desktop1080/4k feel to fix phones.

baseline.reference: Desktop1080 class, full field, no Game Boy/PSP chrome (e.g. matrix 1080p / laptop_hd playing)

desktop_feel_targets.player_css_diameter_px: ~24–40 at common desktop CSS heights (1080p ~36; laptop_hd ~26)

play_area_relative.def: playfield_area / full_canvas_area (CSS or world-consistent). Approx from blue play rect vs full capture frame.
play_area_relative.how: Measure blue field rect vs full PNG (or use PlayBounds half*2 / view_half*2 from code for the format class).

on_screen_entity_size_css_px.def: Apparent diameter of player/hazard/star in CSS pixels: world_diameter * (css_viewport_height / view_height).
on_screen_entity_size_css_px.how: Estimate from playing PNG (player cyan disc) or compute: 2*radius * css_h / view_height_for(class).

cross_field_time_s.def: Time to traverse the short axis of the playfield at PLAYER_SPEED (no dash): short_axis_world / PLAYER_SPEED.
cross_field_time_s.how: short_axis = min(field_width, field_height) in world units from PlayBounds; compare handheld band to desktop ~3.1s.

stick_hit_diameter_css_px.def: Virtual stick outer hit target diameter in CSS px (base/ring, not only knob).
stick_hit_diameter_css_px.how: Measure stick circle on playing PNG / still; or 2*stick_r_world * css_h / view_height.

dash_hit_diameter_css_px.def: DASH button hit diameter in CSS px.
dash_hit_diameter_css_px.how: Same as stick; I-FATTY-FINGER still applies.

hazard_star_density.def: Count of visible hazards (and stars) relative to playfield area on a mid-play still — not only at spawn frame 0.
hazard_star_density.how: Open mid-play e2e still or video frame ≥5s into play; compare packing to desktop Normal difficulty feel.

speed_feel.def: Subjective on-screen motion rate. Same world PLAYER_SPEED is OK if view_height_for compensates so entities are not microscopic and traversal is not snail-like or twitch-only.
speed_feel.how: Sample ≥20s play video; flag if chrome-short landscape makes field postage-stamp + motion feel wrong vs desktop.

bands.notes: Approximate acceptance bands for review judgment. Prefer measuring this-run artifacts; bands are guides not silent auto-PASS.

handheld_cross_time_s.notes: Desktop ~3.1s. Handheld shorter is expected (smaller world field) but <0.85s is unfair twitch; >>2.8s with tiny entities feels sluggish.

required_for_fairness_review: phone_rodin_chrome (or equivalent short-height Chrome browsing portrait) — browser chrome shrinks CSS height; primary resize unfairness repro
At least one phone landscape with short CSS height (e.g. phone_landscape, phone_rodin_landscape, phone_android_landscape)
At least one desktop playing cell for F-NO-DESKTOP-REGRESS (1080p or laptop_hd)
Mid-play stills or video frames with hazards+stars present (not only quality-hold frame 0 of Playing)
recommended: phone_portrait vs phone_rodin_chrome same device class — full CSS vs Chrome tab height
Chrome landscape short height on phone (address bar + thin height) if available in matrix or Phase C landscape_browsing

criteria.check: Playfield is large enough to dodge fairly vs desktop difficulty curve. With Game Boy/PSP chrome, field area fraction of canvas typically ≥0.40 and not a postage-stamp strip. Short axis must leave room for player + hazard clearance (not only HUD).
fail_if: Blue field is so small that Normal-difficulty dodge is obviously harder than desktop solely due to layout (not spawn RNG)
Chrome eats field so only a thin band remains (area fraction roughly <0.40 or short axis unusable)
phone_rodin_chrome / short-height portrait collapses play area vs full-screen portrait unacceptably

criteria.check: Player and hazard on-screen sizes in CSS pixels are readable for the class — not microscopic and not cartoon-huge. Prefer player diam ~24–40 CSS px; never roughly <18 or >52 on phone playing shots.
fail_if: Player/hazard look like noise pixels on landscape short height
Entities fill most of the field so dodge geometry is meaningless
view_height_for not compensating: landscape entities still ~half portrait size unfairly tiny

criteria.check: Time to cross the short play axis at PLAYER_SPEED stays in an acceptable band (~0.85–2.8s). Desktop reference ~3.1s. Handheld may be snappier; must not be pure twitch (<~0.85s) or feel like a crawl with tiny field motion.
fail_if: Short-axis cross time estimated <0.85s on a primary phone format
Cross time high while entities still tiny (sluggish + hard to see)
Video shows player crossing entire field in a blink with no dodge window vs desktop

criteria.check: Virtual stick hit diameter ≥48 CSS px (prefer ≥64). Fortnite-style translucent base + white ring + knob must stay readable and fatty-finger friendly. Knob stays inside ring (SIM-STICK-MAX-DEFLECT).
fail_if: Stick diameter <48 CSS px
Stick unreadable ring/knob on landscape short height
Stick looks OK size but overlaps field (also V-PLAY-CONTROLS-OUTSIDE-FIELD)

criteria.check: DASH button hit diameter ≥48 CSS px (prefer ≥56); gap to stick ≥12 CSS px (I-FATTY-FINGER / SIM-FAT-FINGER-GAP).
fail_if: DASH smaller than ~48 CSS px hit target
Stick and DASH so close mid-tap hits both or neither

criteria.check: Same world speeds are OK if view_height_for and chrome layout keep motion readable. Flag when chrome landscape / short CSS height makes play feel sluggish, tiny, or unfairly twitchy relative to desktop baseline without a compensating layout fix.
fail_if: Entities tiny AND motion hard to track on ≥20s handheld video
Desktop motion feel changed (speed constants / desktop view_height) as a mobile fix
Landscape short-height play feels like a different, broken game vs portrait of same device class

criteria.check: Star/hazard density relative to field area remains fair. Smaller handheld fields must not pack the same absolute clutter into a stamp so Normal is effectively Insane. Judge mid-play stills/video, not only empty early frames.
fail_if: Mid-play handheld field is wall-to-wall hazards with no dodge corridors while desktop Normal still has room
Stars/hazards constantly stacked on HUD only because field is too short (also V-PLAY-HAZARD-NOT-ON-HUD)

criteria.check: Desktop/laptop playing keeps current good feel: full playfield (no Game Boy/PSP chrome), no stick/DASH buttons, entity size and cross-time in desktop band, HUD clear. Mobile-only fixes must not slow desktop, shrink desktop field, or add touch chrome.
fail_if: Stick or DASH appears on desktop playing (also V-PLAY-DESKTOP-NO-STICK)
Desktop field shrunk or view_height changed away from 1080 without product intent
Desktop player/speed feel worse than pre-change baseline on this-run 1080p/laptop playing artifacts

review_checklist_fairness.description: Usability + fairness on playing artifacts. Walk after visual V-PLAY-* checks. Desktop baseline must not regress; handheld uses chrome + view_height_for compensation.
evidence: Mid-play e2e stills or video frames for density + speed feel
review_checklist_fairness.when_to_apply: F-* when: applies only per fairness_usability.criteria when/when desktop|handheld. Desktop playing cells: emphasize F-NO-DESKTOP-REGRESS + F-SPEED-FEEL. Handheld: all F-* except F-NO-DESKTOP-REGRESS is desktop-only.
